consentNote: Listed here because the dossier flagged each as audience leads with public-facing professional profiles (LinkedIn, personal sites, Substacks). To be added or removed, open an issue at github.com/WalksWithASwagger/cmvan-keynote.

profiles.name: Philippe Pasquier
profiles.role: Director, Metacreation Lab for Creative AI
profiles.stance: Practitioner from inside the field. Frames AI as co-creation partner, which complicates the theft narrative in interesting ways. Expect nuanced pushback, not hostility.
profiles.relevance: Most likely to engage hardest on the ethics section. 250+ peer-reviewed papers; consent and training data are live research questions for him, not abstract.

profiles.name: Morten Rand-Hendriksen
profiles.stance: Most ideologically aligned with the talk's themes. Argues AI companies should be required to get active opt-in consent from creators, not rely on opt-out. Anti-anthropomorphization, pro-creator-rights.
profiles.relevance: Wrote the canonical 'AI Training and the Slow Poison of Opt-Out' essay. Educator reach measured in millions. The opt-in consent fight maps directly onto Slide 10.

profiles.name: Tom Froese
profiles.stance: Cautiously pragmatic with real anxiety. Did a full episode 'Surviving the AI Illustration Apocalypse'. Uses AI for admin but not as a creative tool. Publicly wrestling with 'Should You Be Embarrassed About Using AI?'
profiles.relevance: His 120K+ Skillshare students are illustrators who are worried. His Luddite episode mirrors the historical pattern Kris opens with. The 'taste as the moat' frame is exactly what his students need.

profiles.name: Zain Meghji
profiles.stance: Interviewer energy. 20+ years in Canadian TV — Oprah, Beyoncé, Matt Damon. Now grows YouTube channels for major studios. Algorithmic platforms are his day-to-day.
profiles.relevance: Hosts 'Chaotic Creative' podcast — the exact frame as punk-rock-meets-AI. Potential post-talk feature. Amplifier with media instincts.

profiles.name: Sean Cranbury
profiles.stance: Skepticism toward AI discourse and hype, with curiosity as key. Defends analog/tactile culture while working on responsible AI adoption. Substack 'doomscrōll' — the name says everything.
profiles.relevance: Natural ally and already in the orbit (BC+AI). He's doing professionally what Kris is saying from the stage. AI governance consultant for nonprofits, healthcare, arts orgs across BC.

profiles.name: Jai Djwa
profiles.stance: Approaches AI through equity, not productivity or hype. Frames AI as a resource-multiplier for under-resourced organizations.
profiles.relevance: Has spoken on 'AI and Justice'. Will connect to the 'who does AI serve?' thread. The 'Agentic Digital Media' name predates the 'agentic' buzzword — pointed coincidence.

profiles.name: Kemp Edmonds
profiles.role: Director · Co-organizer of Vancouver AI meetup
profiles.stance: Enthusiast and connector. Three words he uses: technology, education, community. Builds the room.
profiles.relevance: Built Hootsuite's training function from 25 to 900+. Created BCIT's first social media courses. Connector for future Vancouver AI creative events.

profiles.name: Bradley Shende
profiles.stance: Multi-award-winning creative technologist. Indigenext is an Indigenous business incubator built on 'Reconciliation through Innovation'. Critically aware of technology's extractive potential.
profiles.relevance: The live crisis his world is navigating: no federal or tribal legislation protects Indigenous data from AI scraping. The consent/theft frame in the talk maps directly onto a policy crisis he lives professionally. 'Data as land.'

profiles.name: Robin Ashmore
profiles.stance: Studio originally from IBM's Center for Advanced Learning — IBM Design's AI ethics frameworks shaped institutional context. 680+ projects, mission to catalyze behavior change at scale.
profiles.relevance: The 'creative liberation' angle lands here. Climate Smart, Living Wage, Accessible Employer certified. Works on traditional Musqueam, Squamish, and Tsleil-Waututh territory.

profiles.name: Marcia Chandra
profiles.stance: Participatory ethnography requires informed consent from subjects. Consent is literally her professional framework. AI image generation trained on scraped photographers is a live professional threat.
profiles.relevance: Master of Environmental Studies. Contributor to openDemocracy. Her 'Who Are We' project work and community-centered visual practice sit in direct tension with AI image scraping.

profiles.name: Yuri Artibise
profiles.stance: Not an AI person — his lane is urban planning and civic engagement. DIY Urbanism, co-op housing advocacy. Regular CM attendee.
profiles.relevance: Bridge to civic / public-policy thinking. Director of VCPC since 2016, advising Council on long-range planning.
